articles.title: Periscope Equity Makes Strategic Investment in Amusement Connect Payments Platform
articles.summary: Private equity firm Periscope Equity has made a strategic investment in Amusement Connect, a specialty payments platform built specifically for the amusement industry. The deal signals growing institutional investor confidence in FEC-focused technology infrastructure. For operators, this investment could accelerate product development and wider adoption of purpose-built payment solutions across the sector.

articles.title: ROLLER Partners with Adyen to Offer Embedded Financing for 3,000+ Venues
articles.summary: Venue management software provider ROLLER has partnered with global payments giant Adyen to offer fast, embedded financing through a Capital product available to more than 3,000 venues on its platform. The move gives FEC operators access to growth capital directly through their existing venue management system, reducing friction in securing funds for expansions or equipment purchases. This kind of embedded fintech offering is becoming a key differentiator for venue software platforms competing for FEC clients.

articles.title: Slick City Action Park Appoints First Chief Marketing Officer Amid 40-Location Expansion
articles.summary: Slick City Action Park, the indoor dry-slide concept, has named Josh Cole as its first-ever Chief Marketing Officer as the brand surpasses 40 open locations across North America and eyes international growth. The hire signals a maturation of the brand's go-to-market strategy as it competes in the increasingly crowded indoor adventure park space. FEC operators should watch Slick City's marketing playbook as it scales, given its rapid unit growth in a proprietary attraction category.

articles.title: Sacoa Installs Cashless Payment Technology at Virginia Pinstack Location
articles.summary: Sacoa has completed a cashless technology installation at a Pinstack location in Virginia, expanding its footprint in the upscale bowling and entertainment center segment. Cashless and card-based redemption systems continue to be a major operational upgrade priority for multi-attraction FECs. The Pinstack deployment offers a real-world reference point for operators evaluating similar system upgrades.

articles.title: Six Flags Expands Flexible Membership Program to Six Additional Parks
articles.summary: Six Flags Entertainment Corporation is rolling out its flexible membership program to six more parks starting June 8, offering guests multi-park access and value-driven annual plans including designated park bundles for Gold Members. The program represents a significant shift in how large regional operators are structuring guest loyalty and recurring revenue. FEC operators should take note of how the membership model is being refined at scale, as similar structures are increasingly being adopted by smaller venue operators.

articles.title: K1 Speed Opens Multi-Level Indoor Go-Kart Facility in Thornton, Colorado
articles.summary: K1 Speed has opened a new indoor amusement center in Thornton, Colorado, featuring a multi-level go-kart track as the anchor attraction. The opening continues K1 Speed's national expansion of its electric indoor karting concept in suburban markets. Multi-level track designs are increasingly being used by karting operators to maximize square footage and throughput in urban and suburban locations.

articles.title: SisyFox Launches Versatile New Pay-Per-Play System for Indoor Leisure Operators
articles.summary: German game studio SisyFox has unveiled a new pay-per-play system designed specifically for indoor leisure and attractions operators, supporting coin systems, credits, RFID wristbands, and ticket dispensers from a single platform. The system allows operators to link physical park infrastructure directly to individual player accounts via RFID, enabling personalized experiences and streamlined monetization. This kind of flexible, low-maintenance payment integration is highly relevant for FEC operators looking to modernize arcade and attraction revenue collection.

articles.title: Urban Air Adventure Park Continues National Expansion with New Maryland Location
articles.summary: Urban Air Adventure Park is expanding its indoor entertainment footprint with a new Maryland location, continuing one of the most aggressive growth trajectories in the trampoline and adventure park segment. The brand's consistent market penetration into new states reflects strong consumer demand for multi-attraction indoor venues. Competing FEC operators and potential franchisees should track Urban Air's site selection strategy as a benchmark for market viability.

articles.title: Urban Air Adventure Park Opens New Location in Salem, Oregon
articles.summary: A new Urban Air Adventure Park has opened in Salem, Oregon, bringing the brand's multi-attraction indoor entertainment model to the Pacific Northwest market. The Salem opening highlights continued franchise-driven expansion into mid-sized U.S. cities. Operators in similar markets should monitor guest response and pricing strategies as Urban Air establishes itself in new regional demographics.

articles.title: Sneak Peek: Urban Air Adventure Park Prepares to Open in Salem, Oregon
articles.summary: Ahead of its official opening, Salem's new Urban Air Adventure Park offered a preview of its multi-attraction layout including trampolines, climbing structures, and other adventure elements. The preview coverage provides useful insight into how the brand positions new venues for local media and community engagement prior to opening. Pre-opening visibility campaigns of this kind are an effective model for any FEC launching in a new market.

articles.title: Spokane Valley Family Entertainment Center Closes After Two Years in Operation
articles.summary: A family entertainment center in Spokane Valley has permanently closed its doors after just two years in business, adding to a pattern of FEC closures that underscore the challenges of sustaining new venues beyond the initial excitement phase. The closure, reported locally as Flight 509, highlights the difficulty operators face in building repeat visitation and stable revenue streams in smaller markets. Industry stakeholders should study the operational and market factors contributing to early-stage FEC failures.

articles.title: Indoor Entertainment Center Signs Lease in Former Sears Space at Sunrise Mall
articles.summary: An indoor entertainment center has signed a lease to occupy part of a former Sears anchor space at Sunrise Mall, reflecting the continued trend of FECs backfilling dead mall retail with experience-based attractions. This type of adaptive reuse is increasingly attractive to both landlords seeking traffic drivers and FEC operators looking for affordable large-format spaces. The deal is a useful case study for operators and developers exploring similar conversion opportunities.

articles.title: Chuck E. Cheese Partners with Asmodee for Spot It! Summer In-Venue Experience
articles.summary: Chuck E. Cheese has teamed up with board game publisher Asmodee to bring a Spot It!-branded summer experience into its venues, blending tabletop IP with the FEC environment. The partnership is an example of how family entertainment centers can leverage licensed game brands to create fresh, low-capital seasonal programming. FEC operators looking for differentiated party and event content should watch how this collab performs through the summer.

articles.title: Five Iron Golf Opens Second Dubai Venue, Expanding Indoor Golf Footprint in Middle East
articles.summary: Five Iron Golf, the U.S.-based indoor golf and entertainment concept, has opened its second location in Dubai's Business Bay district, signaling growing international appetite for tech-enhanced indoor golf experiences. The expansion into the Middle East follows a broader global trend of simulator-based golf venues attracting both casual players and corporate clientele. FEC operators considering golf simulation as an anchor or secondary attraction will find Five Iron's international growth trajectory instructive.

articles.title: Tenpin Bowling and Entertainment Centre Opens in Blackpool
articles.summary: UK-based entertainment chain Tenpin has opened a new venue in Blackpool, expanding its multi-attraction bowling and amusement center model into a major UK leisure destination. The opening reinforces Tenpin's position as one of the most active FEC-style operators in the UK market. Operators and suppliers tracking international FEC growth should note the brand's continued investment in new site development.

articles.title: dvLED vs. Projection for Attractions: A Practical Guide to the Real Trade-Offs
articles.summary: A detailed technical analysis breaks down the five critical factors operators must evaluate when choosing between direct-view LED and projection display technology for attractions: contrast environment, geometry, acoustic transparency, total cost of ownership, and display surface requirements. The piece argues that the marketing pitch for dvLED, while largely accurate, can lead operators to adopt the wrong technology for their specific application. FEC owners investing in immersive experiences, dark rides, or large-format display attractions will find this a highly useful decision-making framework.

articles.title: Masti Zone Opens New Indoor Entertainment Venue in Lucknow, India
articles.summary: Masti Zone, an indoor entertainment brand, has opened a new location in Lucknow as part of its continued expansion across India's growing family entertainment market. The opening reflects accelerating FEC development in South Asian urban markets driven by rising middle-class leisure spending. Suppliers and franchise operators looking at international growth opportunities should monitor the Indian FEC sector closely.

articles.title: Dellshire Resort Opens in Wisconsin Dells with Immersive Dragon Centerpiece Attraction
articles.summary: A new resort in Wisconsin Dells has opened featuring a large fire-breathing dragon as its signature centerpiece attraction, adding a theatrical entertainment element to the region's already competitive family leisure market. Wisconsin Dells is one of the most concentrated FEC and resort markets in the Midwest, making new openings there a useful barometer for attraction differentiation strategies. The dragon feature highlights growing consumer appetite for spectacle-driven, shareable destination moments.

articles.title: Madison, Mississippi Entertainment District 'The Gallery' to Break Ground This Summer
articles.summary: A new entertainment district called The Gallery is set to break ground in Madison, Mississippi this summer, representing a significant mixed-use leisure development for the market. Entertainment districts anchored by FEC-style attractions continue to attract developer and municipal interest as retail-adjacent experience destinations. Operators and investors tracking new venue development pipelines in the Southeast should watch this project.

articles.title: NRG Adventure Park Moves Forward at Jacksonville's Southside Commons Development
articles.summary: NRG Adventure Park is advancing its plans within the Southside Commons development in Jacksonville, Florida, signaling continued momentum for the project in a competitive Southeast FEC market. Adventure park concepts anchored in mixed-use retail and dining developments have shown strong performance, and Jacksonville's growing population makes it an attractive target market. Operators and developers in the region should monitor the project's timeline and attraction mix.

articles.title: RePlay Magazine Party Professor: June 2026 Edition for FEC Party Planning
articles.summary: RePlay Magazine's Party Professor column for June 2026 offers practical guidance for FEC operators on birthday party and group event programming, a critical revenue stream for most family entertainment centers. Party packages consistently rank as one of the highest-margin offerings at FECs, making trade publication guidance on pricing, theming, and upsells particularly valuable. Operators looking to refresh their summer party offerings should consult this resource.

articles.title: Menasha's Sabre Lanes Bowling Alley Permanently Closes
articles.summary: Sabre Lanes in Menasha, Wisconsin has permanently closed after years in operation, with ownership directing bowlers to a sister location in Appleton. The closure is part of a broader trend of standalone bowling alleys struggling to compete against multi-attraction FECs offering bowling alongside arcades, dining, and other entertainment. Independent bowling center operators face continued pressure to diversify their attraction mix or consolidate operations.

articles.title: Ninja Kingdom Indoor Attraction Comes to Delhi Mall This Summer
articles.summary: Nexus Select CITYWALK has brought Ninja Kingdom, a ninja-themed indoor attraction, to its Delhi shopping center for the summer season, reflecting growing demand for pop-up and seasonal indoor entertainment activations in South Asian retail environments. The concept mirrors a strategy used successfully by FEC operators in Western markets to drive mall traffic and create time-limited guest urgency. Operators considering seasonal or pop-up attraction formats as a testing or revenue strategy may find this model worth examining.

articles.title: London-Inspired Indoor Entertainment Concept with Interactive Soccer and Games Coming to Paris
articles.summary: A new indoor entertainment venue featuring foosball, virtual soccer, interactive games, cocktails, and fast food is set to open in Paris, imported from the London market. The concept blends sports entertainment with social gaming and F&B in a format that has proven popular with young adult demographics in the UK. FEC operators evaluating sports-themed social entertainment as an attraction category will find this European expansion a useful market signal.

articles.title: Kids Bowl Free Program Returns at Central Florida Bowling Centers This Summer
articles.summary: Several Central Florida bowling centers are again participating in the Kids Bowl Free summer program, offering complimentary games to children as a traffic-driving strategy during the competitive summer leisure season. The program is a well-established demand generation tool for bowling operators, converting free visits into paid shoe rentals, F&B, and arcade spend. Operators not yet enrolled in similar programs should evaluate the ROI of participation heading into peak summer months.

articles.title: Play CENLA Indoor Entertainment Venue Shuts Down in Louisiana
articles.summary: Play CENLA, a family entertainment venue in Louisiana's Rapides Parish, has shut down operations, adding to a string of recent FEC closures across smaller U.S. markets. The closure underscores the ongoing viability challenges facing independently operated FECs in markets with limited population density and strong seasonal demand swings. Operators and investors should treat closures like this as data points when evaluating site selection and market sizing for new or existing venues.
